title: State’s ratepayer advocate accuses Duke of overcharging customers in appeal to IURC
description:
The state’s top utility customer advocate says Duke Energy Indiana over-collected more than $89 million from its ratepayers and is set to over-collect another $86 million annually.

title: Northwest Indiana RDA approves demolition contract for Genesis Center
description:
The Northwest Indiana RDA said the $2.87 million bid from Chicago-based McDonagh Demolition is part of the city's Blight Elimination Program established by Mayor Eddie Melton in 2023 when he was a state senator.

title: Governor to name Chuck Goodrich as next Indiana commerce secretary
description:
Indiana Gov. Mike Braun plans to name former state lawmaker Chuck Goodrich, CEO and owner of Indianapolis-based Gaylor Electric Inc., to serve as the state’s next commerce secretary.

title: The Vista at Origin Park helps bring new life to former landfill site in Clarksville
description:
The Vista at Origin Park, a $20 million venue for weddings, corporate functions and social events along the Ohio River in Clarksville, is now open after 19 months of construction.

title: Indiana ends summer fuel requirement in Clark, Floyd counties
description:
The Indiana Environmental Rules Board voted unanimously Monday to suspend a rule that requires gas stations in the two counties to sell a lower-emissions gasoline blend during the summer months.

title: Boone County passes moratorium on data center development
description:
The Boone County Commissioners on Monday approved a one-year moratorium on the development of data centers within unincorporated areas of the county.

title: Mount Baldy reopens after sand management project
description:
The National Park Service said the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers moved 27,000 tons of sand to help address sand accumulation at the site, including portions of the parking area and nearby sanitary infrastructure.

title: Federal customs agents seize 1,578 fake World Cup items in Indianapolis
description:
Indianapolis customs officers seized an estimated $134,000 worth of counterfeit World Cup merchandise earlier this month, officials announced Monday.
